name: Tuggle v. Netherland
question: Did the Court of Appeals correctly interpret Zant v. Stephens, 462 U.S. 862, to establish a rule that, in States that do not weigh aggravating circumstances against mitigating circumstances, a death sentence may be upheld on the basis of one valid aggravating circumstance, regardless of the reasons for which another aggravating factor may have been found to be invalid?
